title: Fixing CSS Margin Collapse on Flex Containers

statement.markdown: Write a JavaScript function to fix the margin collapse issue on flex containers in CSS. Given the CSS string, modify the margin values to prevent collapse and return the updated CSS string. The input CSS string is a single line string where all CSS properties are separated by semicolons. The function should handle both margin-top and margin-bottom properties.
statement.inputFormat: A single line string of CSS properties separated by semicolons
statement.outputFormat: The updated CSS string with fixed margin values
statement.notes: The function should handle both margin-top and margin-bottom properties. The input CSS string may contain other properties as well.

examples.explanation: Fixed margin-bottom value to prevent collapse
Fixed margin-bottom value to prevent collapse

hints.text: The margin collapse issue occurs when two or more elements share a common parent and have adjacent margins.

editorial.title: Fixing Margins on Flex Containers
editorial.intuition: To fix the margin collapse issue, we need to set the margin-bottom value to a minimum of the sum of the margin-top and margin-bottom values of adjacent elements.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves parsing the CSS string, identifying the margin properties, and manually updating the values.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution involves using regular expressions to extract the margin properties and update the values accordingly.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a CSS parser library to parse the CSS string and update the margin properties programmatically.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected CSS string should prevent margin collapse and render correctly in the browser.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the optimal solution
commonMistakes: Not handling both margin-top and margin-bottom properties
